members.name: ColorDefault
members.summary: Sets the default color if no texture is assigned to the uniform.

members.name: TextureSource
members.summary: Sets the texture source mode. Used for reading from the screen, depth, or normal_roughness texture.

members.name: TextureType
members.summary: Defines the type of data provided by the source texture.

values.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_BLACK
values.summary: Defaults to fully opaque black color.

values.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ColorDefault] enum.

values.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: Defaults to fully transparent black color.

values.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_WHITE
values.summary: Defaults to fully opaque white color.

values.name: FILTER_DEFAULT
values.summary: Sample the texture using the filter determined by the node this shader is attached to.

values.name: FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAP
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

values.name: FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAP_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant FILTER_LINEAR_MIPMAP] is usually more appropriate in this case.

values.name: FILTER_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureFilter] enum.

values.name: FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel only. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAP
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Use this for non-pixel art textures that may be viewed at a low scale (e.g. due to [Camera2D] zoom or sprite scaling), as mipmaps are important to smooth out pixels that are smaller than on-screen pixels.

values.name: FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAP_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level]. [b]Note:[/b] This texture filter is rarely useful in 2D projects. [constant FILTER_NEAREST_MIPMAP] is usually more appropriate in this case.

values.name: REPEAT_DEFAULT
values.summary: Sample the texture using the repeat mode determined by the node this shader is attached to.

values.name: REPEAT_DISABLED
values.summary: Texture will not repeat.

values.name: REPEAT_ENABLED
values.summary: Texture will repeat normally.

values.name: REPEAT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureRepeat] enum.

values.name: SOURCE_DEPTH
values.summary: The texture source is the depth texture from the depth prepass.

values.name: SOURCE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureSource] enum.

values.name: SOURCE_NONE
values.summary: The texture source is not specified in the shader.

values.name: SOURCE_NORMAL_ROUGHNESS
values.summary: The texture source is the normal-roughness buffer from the depth prepass.

values.name: SOURCE_SCREEN
values.summary: The texture source is the screen texture which captures all opaque objects drawn this frame.

values.name: TYPE_ANISOTROPY
values.summary: Adds [code]hint_anisotropy[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration to use for a flowmap.

values.name: TYPE_COLOR
values.summary: Adds [code]source_color[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration for proper conversion from nonlinear sRGB encoding to linear encoding.

values.name: TYPE_DATA
values.summary: No hints are added to the uniform declaration.

values.name: TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureType] enum.

values.name: TYPE_NORMAL_MAP
values.summary: Adds [code]hint_normal[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration, which internally converts the texture for proper usage as normal map.

constants.name: TYPE_DATA
constants.summary: No hints are added to the uniform declaration.

constants.name: TYPE_COLOR
constants.summary: Adds [code]source_color[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration for proper conversion from nonlinear sRGB encoding to linear encoding.

constants.name: TYPE_NORMAL_MAP
constants.summary: Adds [code]hint_normal[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration, which internally converts the texture for proper usage as normal map.

constants.name: TYPE_ANISOTROPY
constants.summary: Adds [code]hint_anisotropy[/code] as hint to the uniform declaration to use for a flowmap.

constants.name: TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureType] enum.

constants.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_WHITE
constants.summary: Defaults to fully opaque white color.

constants.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_BLACK
constants.summary: Defaults to fully opaque black color.

constants.name: COLOR_DEFAULT_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: Defaults to fully transparent black color.

constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ColorDefault] enum.
